title: Return explicit Meshtastic errors for unsupported app operations
description: Why this exists: the app must fail gracefully when users touch Meshtastic features LICHEN does not implement. What needs to be done: enumerate unsupported MVP operations such as Meshtastic RF settings that do not apply, LoRa channel/radio config writes, admin operations, maps/position updates if not supported, telemetry modules, canned messages, store-and-forward, traceroute/range-test, and remote node operations; implement explicit error/no-op responses rather than silent drops. Acceptance criteria: unsupported command table is recorded; tests cover representative unsupported requests; app receives deterministic error or harmless read-only response; docs Bead project-LICHEN-ev5b.7 can consume the same list.
notes: Implemented no-hardware scope: added compact public unsupported-operation catalog for Meshtastic app compatibility using stable enum IDs plus portnum metadata; catalog covers radio/channel config write no-ops plus every repo-pinned Meshtastic PortNum except TEXT_MESSAGE_APP as supported app-to-node text, including UNKNOWN_APP, app-to-node ROUTING_APP, PRIVATE_APP, ATAK_FORWARDER, and MAX sentinel. Added qemu ztests that assert catalog order/IDs/portnums and exercise all pinned unsupported portnums through lichen_meshtastic_adapter_process_raw(), expecting deterministic queueStatus res=2 with mesh_packet_id and no text side effect. Added Python schema guard parsing rust/lichen-meshtastic/proto/meshtastic/portnums.proto, lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter/src/main.c unsupported_portnums[], and lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c unsupported_operations[] so pinned schema drift fails CI. Review findings fixed: removed misleading raw config/malformed success tests; removed prose strings from firmware API; assigned explicit enum values; included UNKNOWN/ROUTING/MAX; behavior test no longer iterates production catalog; Python guard now checks production catalog too. Quality evidence: git diff --check PASS; cd python && python3 -m ruff check tests/interface/meshtastic/test_zephyr_unsupported_portnums.py PASS; cd python && PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q tests/interface/meshtastic/test_zephyr_unsupported_portnums.py PASS (1 passed); west build -p always -b qemu_x86 lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -d build/meshtastic_adapter_qemu_t2hn9 PASS, RAM 221240 B / 31 MB, expected CONFIG_TIMER_RANDOM_GENERATOR testing warning; qemu run via success-marker wrapper PASS, 45/45 tests, PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL. Reviews: final exact codereviews by Euclid and James found no P0/P1/P2; prior review P2s were fixed before final pass. No EC2 used for this Bead; qemu_x86 ran locally.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope. Added unsupported-operation catalog, qemu/Python drift tests, recorded evidence and review outcomes; upstream newer-portnum refresh split to project-LICHEN-llgw.
